prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Using the current price of AMZN ($204.86), an estimated annualized volatility of 28% (derived from historical data), and an annualized risk-free rate of 5% for drift, the future price distribution for 20 trading days (approximately 0.079 years) is calculated. The point estimate represents the median of this log-normal distribution. The confidence intervals are derived from the standard deviation of log returns over the 20-day period, indicating the range within which the price is expected to fall with the given probability.
